_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: Atlanta City Council batch 1 (Districts 3, 6, 9, 10, 12) — evidence-based local finalization. Roster verified current June 2026: all five re-elected Nov 4, 2025 (Wan and Boone unopposed; Amos 59.25%, Hillis 59.3%, Lewis outright no-runoff). Scores reset to null where no evidence; FALSE only where a documented vote/sponsorship/statement/endorsement exists; TRUE only on affirmative documented alignment (public_justice q0 high bar: advocacy, not routine budget votes).

profile.background: Georgia Tech BIE, Wharton MBA; nonprofit executive (Horizons Atlanta, Jerusalem House). District 6 council member 2010-2018 and again since Jan 2022; re-elected unopposed Nov 2025 to a fourth term. First openly gay man and first Asian American on the Atlanta City Council.

alex-wan.notes_set: Atlanta City Council District 6; re-elected unopposed Nov 4, 2025 (8,610 votes), term to Jan 2030. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence: co-sponsored post-Dobbs 22-R-3711 (no city funds to investigate abortion reports; APD to treat abortion-law violations as lowest priority) and 22-R-3981 (up to $300,000 city donation to the National Network of Abortion Funds) — sanctity_of_life FALSE. Openly gay; endorsed by Georgia Equality and the LGBTQ Victory Fund; voted for the Midtown rainbow crosswalks and publicly worked to make them permanent; rides in Atlanta Pride parades — biblical_marriage FALSE. Voted YES on the Public Safety Training Center funding in committee (May 2023) and in the 11-4 full-council vote (June 6, 2023), saying District 6 constituents 'prioritize supporting public safety'; 2021 platform: raise police pay, more cameras, faster 911 — affirmative advocacy, public_justice q0 TRUE. Voted with the unanimous council (April 2026) for resolutions opposing ICE detention facilities in Atlanta and requiring APD documentation of federal immigration enforcement — border q2 FALSE. Opposed Buckhead cityhood (financial-devastation grounds) — noted, but not scored as a state-mandate cell. No religion publicly identified. NO EVIDENCE found on schools/parental rights, Christian liberty, election administration, or 2A — those cells null, not penalized.

0.note: Co-sponsored post-Dobbs 22-R-3711: no city funds to investigate abortion reports; APD to treat abortion-law violations as lowest possible priority.

1.note: Co-sponsored 22-R-3981 donating up to $300,000 of city funds to the National Network of Abortion Funds; also sponsored a pre-Dobbs council resolution backing abortion rights.

0.note: Openly gay (first openly gay man on the council); endorsed by the LGBTQ Victory Fund and Georgia Equality — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

2.note: Georgia Equality / Victory Fund-endorsed, LGBTQ-affirming public posture.

4.note: Voted for the rainbow crosswalks and publicly pushed to make them permanent ('first priority is to get the project done before Pride'); rides in Atlanta Pride parades.

0.note: Voted YES on Public Safety Training Center funding (committee May 2023 and 11-4 council vote June 2023), publicly citing constituents' priority on public safety; 2021 platform: raise police pay, more cameras, faster 911 — affirmative advocacy, not just budget routine.

2.note: Voted with the unanimous council (April 2026) for resolutions opposing ICE detention facilities in Atlanta and requiring APD documentation of federal immigration-enforcement activity.

profile.religion: Member, Rush Memorial Congregational Church, Atlanta (campaign bio); daughter of civil-rights leader Rev. Joseph E. Boone
profile.background: Longtime southwest Atlanta community figure and former 25-year city/council staffer. District 10 council member since 2018; chairs the Public Safety Committee. Re-elected unopposed Nov 2025.

andrea-boone.notes_set: Atlanta City Council District 10; re-elected unopposed Nov 4, 2025. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence: voted AGAINST the June 2020 ordinance to withhold $73M from APD during the defund push (failed 7-8); voted YES on the Public Safety Training Center land lease (Sept 2021, 10-4) and YES on the $67M funding (June 2023, 11-4); as Public Safety chair shepherded the $10M APD pay-raise package and backs hiring 2,000 officers — public_justice q0 TRUE. Counter-signal, honestly scored: co-sponsored a resolution opposing the Georgia General Assembly's HB 340 preemption of Atlanta's 2018 cash-bail elimination — defended cashless bail (public_justice q1 FALSE) while asserting local control against a state mandate (refuse_state_overreach q0 TRUE). Campaign bio claims balancing the budget yearly 'without increasing taxes' — routine-governance claim, econ cell left null. Member, Rush Memorial Congregational Church. NO EVIDENCE found on life, marriage/gender, schools, elections, immigration, or 2A — those cells null, not penalized (her individual vote on the post-Dobbs abortion resolutions was not locatable).

0.note: Voted against the 2020 ordinance withholding $73M from APD; YES on the training-center lease (2021) and $67M funding (2023); shepherded the $10M APD pay raise as Public Safety chair; supports hiring 2,000 officers.

1.note: Co-sponsored a resolution opposing state preemption (HB 340) of Atlanta's 2018 elimination of cash bail for low-level offenses — defended the cashless-bail policy.

0.note: Co-sponsored the resolution opposing the General Assembly's HB 340 preemption of city bail policy — an explicit assertion of local control against a state mandate (note: in defense of bail reform, scored FALSE on public_justice q1).

profile.background: Fifth-generation Atlantan; former Congressional Black Caucus staffer on police-accountability issues. Unseated incumbent Joyce Sheperd in the 2021 runoff; District 12 member since Jan 2022; re-elected outright Nov 2025 (term to Jan 2030). Chairs Community Development/Human Services.

antonio-lewis.notes_set: Atlanta City Council District 12; re-elected outright Nov 4, 2025, defeating Stephanie Flowers and Delvin Davis — no runoff. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence: co-sponsored the 2022 council resolution endorsing 'the fundamental right to abortion' and urging the legislature to ensure access — sanctity_of_life FALSE. One of 4 NO votes on the $67M Public Safety Training Center funding (June 2023, 11-4); voted against $1.7M additional training-center security funding (Nov 2024); told APD officers on patrol 'Always say not guilty when you go to court... Police officers lie!' (Sept 2024, no regrets expressed) — public_justice q0 FALSE. Lead sponsor of 26-R-3363 (passed April 2026) directing APD to document federal immigration-enforcement activity and retain bodycam footage of ICE operations, stating the goal is to 'protect immigrants and minorities,' modeled on Chicago — border q2 FALSE. Initiated a free gun lock-box safety program with APD (2024) — safety programming, not a carry/ban position; 2A cells null. Self-describes as a 'child of God' on social media; no church documented. NO EVIDENCE on marriage/gender, schools, Christian liberty policy, fiscal restraint, or elections — null, not penalized.

0.note: Co-sponsored the 2022 council resolution endorsing 'the fundamental right to abortion' as SCOTUS weighed Dobbs.

1.note: Same resolution urged the Georgia Legislature to ensure abortion access — advocacy contrary to abolition.

0.note: One of 4 NO votes on the 2023 training-center funding; voted against 2024 security funding; told APD officers 'Police officers lie!' on bodycam (Sept 2024) and said he had no regrets.

2.note: Lead sponsor of 26-R-3363 (April 2026): APD must document federal immigration-enforcement activity and retain bodycam footage of ICE operations; stated goal to 'protect immigrants and minorities,' modeled on Chicago.

profile.religion: Baptist — trustee, Beulah Baptist Church, Vine City (campaign bio)
profile.background: Native Atlantan, lifelong Vine City resident; owns a private security company. Former Atlanta Board of Education member (District 2) and NPU-L chair. Won District 3 in 2021 by 45 votes; re-elected Nov 2025 (59.25%) over Perrin Bostic. B.S., Central Michigan University.

byron-amos.notes_set: Atlanta City Council District 3; re-elected Nov 4, 2025, 2,773-1,907 (59.25%), term to Jan 2030. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence: co-sponsored post-Dobbs 22-R-3711 (no city funds to record/investigate abortion reports; lowest APD priority) — sanctity_of_life FALSE. On the APS school board voted NO on KIPP charter-school expansion (Dec 2017) and NO on KIPP charter renewals/leases — contrary to school choice, family q0 FALSE (campaign site also touts championing APS's first Equity Policy — noted, not scored). Voted YES on the $67M Public Safety Training Center funding (June 2023, 11-4; 'the majority of my community leaders... wanted me to vote yes'); 2021 questionnaire called for APD to reach 2,000 active officers plus more cameras/license-plate readers; proposed nightclub security-plan ordinance; held Vine City pressers demanding more APD action on gun violence — public_justice q0 TRUE. Oct 2025: Atlanta must 'defend our home-rule authority, especially on issues like housing, public safety, and environmental regulation' — subsidiarity affirmation, refuse_state_overreach q3 TRUE. Baptist (Beulah Baptist trustee). NO EVIDENCE on marriage/gender (the 2011 school-board mailer controversy was denied by him — not scored), parental consent, elections, immigration (his individual vote on the ICE resolutions not documented), or 2A — null, not penalized.

0.note: Co-sponsored post-Dobbs 22-R-3711: no city funds to record/investigate abortion-care reports; APD to place abortion-related reports at lowest possible priority.

1.note: Same co-sponsorship — de-facto non-enforcement advocacy, contrary to abolition.

0.note: As an APS school-board member voted NO on KIPP charter-school expansion (Dec 2017) and NO on KIPP charter renewals/leases — documented votes against school-choice expansion.

Voted YES on the 2023 training-center funding; called for APD to reach 2,000 active officers; proposed nightclub security-screening ordinance; publicly demanded more APD action on Vine City gun violence.

3.note: Oct 2025: Atlanta must 'defend our home-rule authority, especially on issues like housing, public safety, and environmental regulation' — affirmation of decisions at the lowest competent level.

profile.background: ICU/registered nurse (Grady, later Emory); from McMinnville, TN; degrees from MTSU, Georgia State, Valdosta State. District 9 member since 2018; chaired Public Safety 2018-19, now vice-chair. Re-elected Nov 2025 (59.3%) over Charles Bourgeois, term to Jan 2030.

dustin-hillis.notes_set: Atlanta City Council District 9; re-elected Nov 4, 2025, 59.3%-40.7%, no runoff. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] HARD evidence: co-sponsored post-Dobbs 22-R-3711 (abortion-report investigations defunded/deprioritized), 22-R-3981 (up to $300,000 city funds to the National Network of Abortion Funds), and 19-R-4931 (creating a 'Reproductive Justice Commission') — sanctity_of_life FALSE. Endorsed by the Georgia Stonewall Democrats (LGBTQ Democratic org) — biblical_marriage q4 FALSE (accepted LGBTQ-org endorsement; no marriage/trans-specific statements found, q0/q2 null). Sponsored an ordinance to DOUBLE the parks property-tax millage (0.5 to 1 mill, ~$11.2M/yr) — economic_stewardship FALSE. Strongly pro-police: voted against the 2020 defund ordinance; voted YES on the 2021 training-center lease ('a new fire and police training facility is direly needed'); personally INTRODUCED the 2023 training-center funding legislation; endorsed by the IBPO Atlanta police union; called Chief Schierbaum 'the best police chief in the Southeast, if not the US' — public_justice q0 TRUE. On Buckhead cityhood: undecided but called the secession committee's study a 'sham' — not scored. His individual stance on the April 2026 ICE resolutions was not identified — border null. No public profession of faith found (two pastors endorsed him). NO EVIDENCE on schools/parental rights, Christian liberty, elections, or 2A — null, not penalized.

0.note: Co-sponsored post-Dobbs 22-R-3711 (defund/deprioritize abortion-report investigations) and 19-R-4931 creating a 'Reproductive Justice Commission.'

1.note: Co-sponsored 22-R-3981 donating up to $300,000 of city funds to the National Network of Abortion Funds — advocacy contrary to abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by the Georgia Stonewall Democrats (LGBTQ Democratic organization) — accepted LGBTQ-org endorsement signals support for LGBTQ-affirming public policy.

2.note: Sponsored an ordinance to double the parks property-tax millage (0.5 to 1 mill, ~$11.2M/yr more) — a tax increase, contrary to fiscal restraint.

0.note: Voted against the 2020 defund ordinance; YES on the 2021 training-center lease ('direly needed'); introduced the 2023 funding legislation; IBPO police-union endorsed; publicly praised the police chief.
